expectations.text: Output begins with the literal line `Findings:`
expectations.evidence: Line 1: '# PR Review: `mark task complete` endpoint' — no `Findings:` header

expectations.text: All eight preamble fields are present in order
expectations.evidence: Output uses prose section headings ('## URL design', '## Idempotency'); none of the eight preamble fields appear
task-103 is not mentioned anywhere in the output

expectations.text: Findings are tagged with severity in backticks (`High`, `Medium`, `Low`, or `Critical` if a higher tier is used)
expectations.evidence: No backtick severity tags appear on any finding; severity is implied by section heading order

expectations.text: The use of POST sub-resource verb (`/api/tasks/:id/complete`) is identified as inconsistent with REST conventions — should be PATCH on the resource
expectations.evidence: Section 'URL design — use `PATCH` on the resource': 'sub-resource verb endpoint. The standard REST way ... is a partial update on the task itself ... PATCH /api/tasks/:id'

expectations.text: The missing Idempotency-Key on the side-effectful POST is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Section 'Idempotency — required on a side-effectful POST': 'Add an `Idempotency-Key` header requirement'

expectations.text: The snake_case wire field `task_id` is identified as a finding (should be camelCase)
expectations.evidence: Section 'Wire naming — use camelCase': '`task_id` is snake_case ... `taskId`'

expectations.text: The `200` status (rather than reflecting the state-transition correctly) and the bypass of the canonical error envelope are noted where relevant
expectations.evidence: Section 'Status code' addresses 200/PATCH semantics; section 'Error handling' explicitly notes bypass of the canonical error envelope

expectations.text: Findings are ordered by severity, highest first
expectations.evidence: Higher-impact issues (URL design, idempotency, transactional integrity, authz) appear before naming nits and input-validation polish; ordering is roughly by severity

expectations.text: Output does not cite either skill by name (no mention of 'structured-code-review' or 'rest-api-design' as the source of any rule)
expectations.evidence: Neither skill name appears in the output
